name: Fake Urgency
summary: Countdown timers, 'offer expires soon' messaging, and time-pressure tactics not tied to a real deadline.

evidence.claim: Fake urgency is listed in Brignull's canonical deceptive-patterns taxonomy under Pressure Tactics.

body.description: Countdown timers that reset when the page is reloaded, 'deal ends in 2 hours' claims that renew daily, 'flash sale' pricing that's actually the standard price — all designed to pressure the user into buying before the fake window closes (E1).

detectionHeuristics.description: Record the timer's displayed value; reload and compare. Persistent reset across reloads indicates theater.

body.llmVerifyPrompt: Given a product page with urgency messaging, classify the urgency as (a) real (tied to a stated end datetime that would fail a reasonable disclosure test), (b) rolling-reset (countdown resets on reload / daily), or (c) generic urgency copy without a timer. Return verdict.
body.remediation: Warn the user that urgency framing is often theatrical. Capture the original price for the price-history pack (when available) to check whether the 'deal' price is really a discount.
